Definition: Hard gate or runtime invariant is implemented, tested, and has proof evidence.
Hard gate is true only for the named boundary/scope; not a whole-system claim.
Implemented or specified in part, but not fully proven or not end-to-end authoritative.
Detected or measured, but not yet enforced as a gate/control-flow rule.
Known issue/failure class requiring root-cause closure.
Root cause fixed and proof evidence exists.
Specified or known work that has not been implemented.
Cannot progress until listed dependency/RCA is addressed.
Cause cannot be assigned yet; requires classification before fix can be treated as final.
